questions.question: Highlight a sentence or phrase from the text that supports your answer to Part A.
Part A: Which of the following abilities does the narrator mainly believe older people have that younger people don't?

responses.content: She was disgusted, mad and started crying. She pushed everything that was on her desk away from the sweater.

questions.question: Part A: What is the main conflict Rachel faces in the story?

responses.content: D. She is forced to wear a sweater that isn't hers and can't effectively stand up for herself.

questions.question: Part B: Which evidence from the text BEST shows how Rachel responds to this conflict?

responses.content: A. “That's when everything I've been holding in since this morning, since when Mrs. Price put the sweater on my desk, finally lets go, and all of a sudden I'm crying in front of everybody.”

questions.question: What claim does Sylvia make about the sweater in paragraph 9?

responses.content: It’s not her sweater and its all old and ugly

questions.question: How does Rachel react when Sylvia makes this claim?

responses.content: Sylvia thinks that the sweater is Rachel’s but its not hers

questions.question: Highlight a sentence from paragraphs 18-19 that describes Rachel's physical reaction to wearing the sweater.

responses.content: She wants to say its not hers but can’t get the words out

questions.question:
How does Rachel feel when Sylvia says the sweater belongs to her, and why does she feel this way? 

Respond to this question in the form of a paragraph that includes a claim, evidence from paragraphs 8-11, and explanation.

responses.content: She feels like she is gonna cry because the sweater is not hers and it’s old and ugly it says so in paragraph 18 and 19
She feels like she is gonna cry because the sweater is not hers and it’s old and ugly it says so in paragraph 18 and 19
She feels like she is gonna cry because the sweater is not hers and it’s old and ugly it says so in paragraph 18 and 19

questions.question:
Re-read the following line from paragraph 14:

“Except when math period ends Mrs. Price says loud and in front of everybody, ‘Now, Rachel, that's enough,’ because she sees I've shoved the red sweater to the tippy-tip corner of my desk and it's hanging over the edge like a waterfall, but I don't care.”

What specific characteristic do waterfalls and the hanging sweater share that makes this comparison effective?

responses.content: She compares it to a waterfall

questions.question: In paragraph 19, Rachel says she's “crying like I'm three in front of everybody.” This comparison is an example of:

responses.content: It’s at the tippy top corner of the desk
